title: Determine the exact value of R(B14, B14)
micro_paper_assessment: Not solved yet. The current run proves that any 57-vertex obstruction must be a strongly regular graph with parameters (57,28,13,14), so the exact value question reduces to one rigid conference-graph existence problem. That is a real theorem slice, but it is still too thin for the micro-paper lane until existence or nonexistence of that graph is settled.
generalization_signal: high; the same Goodman pinching should reduce diagonal Bm one-gap obstructions on 4m+1 vertices to conference parameters (4m+1,2m,m-1,m)
candidate_theorem_slice: Any 57-vertex graph with no B14 and with complement containing no B14 must be a strongly regular graph with parameters (57,28,13,14). Equivalently, R(B14, B14) equals 58 exactly when srg(57,28,13,14) exists.
next_action: Either prove nonexistence of srg(57,28,13,14) by a local conference-graph/design argument, or import and verify an explicit 57-vertex graph with those exact parameters.
